paper_title: LandCover6k and LULC Implementation in Earth System Models

methods.description: An iterative approach to incorporate archaeological data into LULC scenarios, revising existing scenarios with diverse archaeological inputs and testing for plausibility and consistency with other evidence.

steps.description: Revising the existing LULC scenarios through the incorporation of diverse archaeological inputs.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate archaeological data into LULC scenarios

steps.description: Testing the revised LULC scenarios for their plausibility and consistency with other lines of evidence.
steps.searchable_summary: validate LULC scenarios against independent evidence

steps.description: Comparing revised LULC scenarios with independent data on land cover changes, specifically pollen-based reconstructions.
steps.searchable_summary: compare LULC scenarios with pollen-based reconstructions

steps.description: Conducting sensitivity tests using global climate models and global carbon cycle models.
steps.searchable_summary: perform sensitivity tests with climate and carbon cycle models

methods.method_name: Implementation of LULC in Earth System Model Simulations
methods.description: A series of simulations to examine the impact of LULC using revised LULC scenarios, building on existing climate model experiments.

steps.description: Propose midHoloceneLULC experiment following CMIP6-PMIP4 protocol with dynamic vegetation.
steps.searchable_summary: run midHoloceneLULC experiment with dynamic vegetation

steps.description: Run transient climate simulation for a longer period of the Holocene starting from midHolocene simulation.
Compare simulated CO2 emissions with ice core records.
steps.searchable_summary: validate CO2 emissions against ice core records

steps.description: Run additional sensitivity tests to account for changes in atmospheric dust loading, solar irradiance, and volcanic forcing.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct sensitivity tests for additional forcings
